issues: item height significantly exceeds 75% of player (keys ~85px tall vs 32px player reference)
elongated key silhouettes read more as decorative wallpaper elements than grounded toy-diorama props
no player reference visible in composite scene making exact ratio hard to confirm
reasoning: The key_boss_v01 asset appears as three tall pixel-art key/staff objects composited into the dungeon hub scene. Each key is roughly 15-20px wide but ~85px tall — well above the 50-75% of player (32px) height budget for items. Width is within spec but vertical extent is approximately 2.5-3x player height, violating the item size rule. The display context (quest-relic showcase panel with arrow) partially mitigates the concern since this may be an intentional UI highlight rather than a free-roaming world prop, but taken as an in-scene asset the height is non-compliant. Perspective is consistent with the top-down 2D style and the asset doesn't overwhelm the scene visually, but the toy-diorama feel is weakened. No creature/summon category errors; this is an item. Score 5 — marginal, needs height reduction to ~16-24px or explicit framing as UI-only collectible display.
